items.prompt:
Read the sentences from the text.
When it comes to coins, I'm a master detective—if I do say so myself (and I do). And my favorite cases always involve my favorite items: coins!
What does the underlined phrase “I’m a master detective” suggest about the author of the text?

options.text: The author is a detective as well as a coin collector.
The author likes to investigate everything about coins.
The author is hired to research information about coins.
The author has reached the highest level in the world of coin collecting.

items.prompt: How does the author’s point of view develop over the course of the text?

options.text: The author’s tone changes from enthusiast to expert.
The author’s style changes from informative to persuasive.
The author reveals that he favors ancient coins to modern day ones.
The author’s opinion of coin collectors changes from positive to negative.

items.prompt: The author used sets of bullet points in the text. What are the most likely reasons why the author structured the text this way? Select two options.

options.text: to explain why people collect coins
to present information chronologically
to organize information in order of importance
to emphasize the reasons why people collect coins
to make the text stand out from similar informational texts
to convey a large amount of information in a clear, concise way

items.prompt: What inference can be made about the author’s opinion on coin collecting? Use evidence from the text to support your answer.

The following question has two parts. First, answer part A. Then, answer part B.

Part A

Read the paragraph from the text and the directions that follow.
Each year, the United States Mint makes between 14 and 20 billion coins that circulate (get passed around when buying and selling), and a bunch more that don't circulate because they are made to be collected. The circulating coins are all made at the United States Mint branches in Philadelphia and Denver. The branches in San Francisco and West Point specialize in coins that don't circulate.
What is the central idea of the paragraph?

options.text: The US Mint has four branches, all of which contribute to coin circulation.
Some US Mint branches make coins for circulation, while other branches make coins for collecting.
Coin collectors benefit from so many coins in circulation from the Philadelphia and Denver branches.
Fewer coins are stamped at the San Francisco and West Point branches than at the Philadelphia and Denver branches.

items.prompt:
Part B

Which detail from the text best supports your answer in part A?

options.text: The US Mint circulates 14 to 20 billion coins each year.
Many coins from the US Mint don’t circulate because they are made for collecting.
San Francisco, Denver, West Point, and Philadelphia all house branches of the US Mint.
San Francisco and West Point Mint locations specialize in coins that don’t circulate, whereas Philadelphia and Denver specialize in those that do circulate.

items.prompt:
Read the sentence from the text.
Each year, the United States Mint makes between 14 and 20 billion coins that circulate (get passed around when buying and selling), and a bunch more that don't circulate because they are made to be collected.
What does the use of the word “Mint” suggest? Select two options.

options.text: a collector of rare coins
a large amount of money
a stamp used to press money
an object in excellent condition
an agency under federal authority
a coiner and distributor of money
